name: Lightened Maple Syrup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart saucepan, combine the maple syrup with 4-1/2 cups of water and bring to a simmer.
2. In a small bowl, mix the remaining one cup of water with kuzu or cornstarch.
3. Remove the syrup from heat and whisk in the kuzu mixture until fully incorporated.
4. Return the mixture to heat and simmer for about 5 minutes to thicken.
5. Serve warm or at room temperature. Refrigerate any unused portion.
This recipe makes approximately 9 cups. For every 1/4 cup serving, there are 93 calories, 23 grams of protein, 5 grams of sodium, 1 gram of carbohydrates, 0 grams of fat, and 0 milligrams of cholesterol. Calcium content is 10 mg. Note: Arrowroot should not replace kuzu in this recipe as it may result in a gelatinous texture.
